channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🌍 Overnight Attacks Rattle U.S.-Iran Ceasefire | The War Zone
item.description: The latest round of tit-for-tat attacks between the U.S. and Iran are among the most severe since a ceasefire was reached on April 8. The post Overnight Attacks Rattle U.S.-Iran Ceasefire appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Qatari-donated Air Force One now sports red, white and blue paint job | Defense News
item.description: The interim Air Force One VC-25B Bridge aircraft is undergoing its final modifications ahead of a summer rollout.

item.title: 📍 ICEYE, Rheinmetall form German joint venture for space-based ISR | Breaking Defense
item.description: The new venture, Rheinmetall ICEYE Space Solutions, includes four German space startups as “initial” partners.

item.title: ⚛️ Germany’s Cobra 600 Is A Jet Powered Interceptor Drone That Slings An IRIS-T Missile | The War Zone
item.description: The Cobra 600 carries the missile hundreds of miles from its runway launch point, extending the reach of surface-to-air missile batteries. The post Germany’s Cobra 600 Is A Jet Powered Interceptor Drone That Slings An IRIS-T Missile appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 Europe Plans to Crack Down on Russia—but for Real This Time | Foreign Policy
item.description: Trump and the Iran war gave Russia a boost. Brussels is fed up.

item.title: 📍 The Geopolitics of the World Cup | Foreign Policy
item.description: How immigration, trade, and conflict are overshadowing soccer.

item.title: 📍 House appropriators release $1 trillion defense bill for FY27 | Breaking Defense
item.description: The bill also restores funding for the E-7 Wedgetail after Defense Department leaders signaled their intent to continue the program.

item.title: 📍 What Comes After Global Order | Foreign Policy
item.description: Rather than a new international framework, we need a system for moving from one framework to the next.

item.title: 🕵️ Drone wingmen face off at Berlin Air Show in race for German CCA | Breaking Defense
item.description: Several full-sized CCA aircraft or models are on display here, with company officials eager to talk up the latest updates.

item.title: 📍 Germany welcome in GCAP, but new Leonardo boss warns about timing | Defense News
item.description: CEO Lorenzo Mariani said that the advantages of a new partner were extra cash and know-how.

item.title: 🏛️ US-Central African Republic Deportation Agreement Escalates Attack on Immigrants and Puts Lives at Risk | Just Security
item.description: Congress should demand transparency and require the U.S. government to publicly release third-country deportation agreements, including with the Central African Republic. The post US-Central African Republic Deportation Agreement Escalates Attack on Immigrants and Puts Lives at…

item.title: 📍 Exporting U.S. Military AI Won’t Be Easy | Foreign Policy
item.description: Middle powers have alternatives, and AI labs have opinions.

item.title: 🌏 Progressives Should Show Real Solidarity With China | Foreign Policy
item.description: Don’t let Chinamaxxing come at the expense of meaningful engagement with Chinese workers and activists.

item.title: 📍 Will We Know if AI Takes Over? Q&A with Benjamin Boudreaux | RAND
item.description: What happens if we off-load our ability to make decisions, to shape the future, to machines? In this Q&A, policy researcher Benjamin Boudreaux discusses how AI could erode human agency over time—and what humans can do to stay in control.

item.title: 🏛️ More F-35s? ‘Fifth-gen-plus’? Germany explores fighter options after FCAS collapse | Breaking Defense
item.description: At the Berlin Air Show, Germany’s air force chief said Germany will need to “pick up” a fifth-gen-plus system by 2035.

item.title: 🌍 German air show opens under shadow of Iran war, fighter project collapse | Defense News
item.description: Airbus, which represented Germany and Spain in FCAS, is increasingly looking to Sweden’s Saab as a preferred partner, according to sources.

item.title: 📍 A Death in Detention Puts Tajikistan’s Police on Trial | The Diplomat
item.description: Six Dushanbe officers face a rare prosecution over the death of Maksudjon Saidov in custody but none of them is charged with torture.

item.title: 🏴 From diversification to integration: A market-based LNG coordination mechanism in Europe | Atlantic Council
item.description: Europe successfully diversified its LNG supply in a short period, but long-term energy security requires a structural redesign of how European gas markets are organized, priced, and supplied. The post From diversification to integration: A market-based LNG coordination mechanism…

item.title: 📍 Rebuilding Trust in Global Biosecurity | RAND
item.description: Global capacity to sequence a new pathogen or deliver a vaccine is greater than ever. But mounting a coordinated response to future biosecurity threats will require a framework designed with geopolitics in mind.

item.title: 📍 Combatting AI Coercion and the Unexpected Climate Dividend | Just Security
item.description: Globally, AI infrastructure is consolidating faster than governments are moving. Governments need to build resilience through diplomatic initiatives and AI partnerships. The post Combatting AI Coercion and the Unexpected Climate Dividend appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🌏 The US usually sells weapons to Taiwan – with drones, expect the reverse | Defense News
item.description: Taiwan's biggest appeal is the lack of China in the manufacturing supply chain, one analyst said.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: June 10, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE U.S. forces yesterday launched retaliatory strikes against Iran following the downing of an Army…

item.title: 🌏 Ethnic Restaurants and Immigrant Entrepreneurs Facing Crisis Under Japan’s New Visa Rules | The Diplomat
item.description: While the government views the new rules as a necessary measure to curb abuse, some observers worry that they may also deter genuine entrepreneurs.

item.title: 🕵️ PHOTOS: The sights of Berlin Air Show 2026 on Day 1 | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Berlin Air Show has officially kicked off, with plenty to see.

item.title: 📍 Dusting a Dirt Road: How The United States Can Break the Cycle of Failing Military Infrastructure | War on the Rocks
item.description: Winter Storm Uri ripped through Texas in January 2021. The frigid temperatures froze pipes, which then burst and caused flooding in aging barracks at Fort Hood, many of which were overdue for renovations and had vulnerable mechanical and utility systems. The burst pipes, damaged…

item.title: 🔐 Cascading Failure: The Spanish Navy’s Reserve Squadron and the Tragedy of Unpreparedness | War on the Rocks
item.description: It is in long periods of peace that victories and defeats are prepared. Peace is the period for men of government, just as war is for men of action. Without the calm preparation carried out by the former, the courage and energy of the latter are of no use. — Captain First Class…

item.title: 📍 Lockheed pitches HIMARS for France, with 18-month timeline offer | Breaking Defense
item.description: France has allocated approximately €600 million ($692 million) to replace its legacy LRU launchers.

item.title: 📍 How to lose friends and alienate allies: Trump’s approach to Oman | ASPI Strategist
item.description: President Donald Trump’s 27 May threat to ‘blow up’ Oman if it didn’t fall into line was strategically self-defeating. With one off-the-cuff remark, the United States risked damaging the one quiet, trusted channel through which ...

item.title: 🛡️ Why Cambodia and Thailand’s Peace Is So Uneasy | Foreign Policy
item.description: A step forward in the maritime dispute masks dangers on the land border.

item.title: 🌏 The Fault Lines in China’s Power | Foreign Affairs
item.description: America must build—and use—leverage against Beijing.
